title: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
hook: Imagine an AI that learns to answer questions by browsing the web like you do.
promise: You will understand how WebGPT trains language models to browse, cite, and improve answers using human feedback.
prerequisite_note: Bring curiosity about how AI can search the web and learn from people’s preferences.

glossary.plain: A system that predicts and generates text based on patterns in language.
Ratings or choices from people that guide how an AI learns or improves.
References to specific online sources that support an answer.
A training method where the AI is rewarded for outputs that people prefer.
A simulated web browser where the AI can search and click links.
The task of generating answers to questions, often using outside information.

cards.title: AI learns to browse
body: Discover how WebGPT taught language models to search, cite, and improve answers using real web browsing and human feedback.

cards.title: Can AI really look things up?
body: Most language models answer questions by guessing from memory, not by searching the web for evidence.
WebGPT set out to show that AI can learn to browse, find sources, and give answers that humans actually trust.

cards.title: How WebGPT answers questions
body: WebGPT starts with a question, then uses a browser to search, click, and read web pages.
It collects information, generates an answer, and attaches citations to show where facts came from.
cards.caption: WebGPT combines web browsing and human feedback to generate answers with references.

cards.title: Guessing vs. searching
body: Compare how a language model answers without browsing versus with WebGPT’s browser-assisted method.
cards.offNote: Relies on training data, may be outdated or inaccurate, and offers no sources.
cards.onNote: Searches live web, cites sources, and usually gives more up-to-date and trustworthy answers.

cards.title: Letting language models browse
body: WebGPT connects a language model to a virtual browser, letting it search, follow links, and read web pages just like a person.
The model learns not just to answer, but to back up its claims with real citations, guided by what humans prefer.

cards.title: Speed vs. trustworthiness
body: Slide between quick, memory-based answers and slower, sourced answers from web browsing.
cards.low: Answers come instantly but may be outdated or wrong.
cards.mid: Some browsing and citations, balancing speed and evidence.
cards.high: Thorough web search, strong citations, but more time per answer.

cards.title: What does WebGPT need?
body: Pick the six key ingredients for a browser-assisted, human-aligned question-answering agent.

choices.label: Language model
choices.note: Generates text and reads web pages.

choices.label: Browser environment
choices.note: Lets the model search and navigate websites.

choices.label: Human feedback
choices.note: Teaches the model which answers people prefer.

choices.label: Citation mechanism
choices.note: Attaches sources to specific claims in answers.

choices.label: Reinforcement learning
choices.note: Improves the model using rewards from feedback.

choices.label: Web search tools
choices.note: Helps the model find relevant information online.

cards.title: WebGPT’s results: more trusted answers
body: Human judges preferred WebGPT’s answers to those from models without browsing or citations.
WebGPT’s answers were more accurate, more up-to-date, and more likely to include useful sources.
cards.caption: WebGPT set a new standard for trustworthy, sourced AI answers.

cards.title: Why WebGPT changed the field
body: WebGPT showed that language models do not have to be static encyclopedias. They can learn to search and cite like real researchers.
This approach inspired later systems, including ChatGPT and others, to blend browsing, citations, and human guidance for better answers.

recap.label: Search
recap.note: Model browses the web for information.

recap.label: Read
recap.note: It reads and summarizes relevant content.

recap.label: Cite
recap.note: Attaches sources to support its answers.

recap.label: Learn
recap.note: Improves using human feedback on answer quality.

cards.title: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
body: Read the full paper for details on training, experiments, and the impact of browser-based AI.
